fullPlan.projectDescription: Replace the current in-memory job queue (src/queue/memory.ts, Map<jobId,Job>) with a Redis-backed queue supporting at-least-once delivery, dead-letter handling, and configurable retry. Producer/consumer API contract preserved so existing callers compile and function unchanged. Goals: durability + horizontal scale (queue survives process restart, multiple consumer instances).
nonGoals: Do not change the producer/consumer API — existing callers must compile without modification
Do not introduce new authentication or credentials beyond the existing REDIS_URL environment variable
Do not remove the in-memory implementation — keep behind a feature flag for rollback
Do not support exactly-once delivery — at-least-once with idempotency_key is sufficient
Do not implement priority queues, scheduled (delayed) jobs, or cron-like features in this project
Do not implement DLQ replay or requeue API — 14-day TTL passive retention is sufficient

phases.title: QueueInterface contract + MemoryQueue conformance
phases.description: Extract the implicit producer/consumer API from memory.ts into an explicit QueueInterface<T> in src/queue/types.ts. Update MemoryQueue to implement it. Export from src/queue/index.ts. This phase locks the contract all subsequent phases must satisfy.
acceptanceCriteria: QueueInterface<T> exported from src/queue/types.ts with: enqueue(job:T):Promise<string>, dequeue():Promise<T|null>, ack(jobId:string):Promise<void>, nack(jobId:string):Promise<void>, size():Promise<number>
MemoryQueue<T> implements QueueInterface<T> and tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors
dequeue() returns null (not undefined, not throws) when queue is empty
src/queue/index.ts re-exports QueueInterface and MemoryQueue
Unit tests cover enqueue→dequeue→ack round-trip and empty-queue null return

executorDecides: Generic type param name
Internal data structure for inflight tracking in MemoryQueue
Test file location and naming convention
Whether ack/nack on unknown jobId no-ops or throws
escalateToOperator: Adding methods beyond the five listed
Changing method signatures in a way that breaks existing callers
Making any method synchronous if callers rely on Promise chaining

phases.title: createQueue factory + QUEUE_BACKEND feature flag
phases.description: Add a createQueue<T>(config?) factory to src/queue/index.ts that reads QUEUE_BACKEND env var and routes to MemoryQueue or a stub RedisQueue. The stub satisfies the type checker but throws 'redis backend not yet implemented' at runtime. This is the cutover switch.
acceptanceCriteria: createQueue<T>() exported from src/queue/index.ts
QUEUE_BACKEND=memory returns a MemoryQueue instance
QUEUE_BACKEND=redis returns a RedisQueue instance (stub is acceptable)
QUEUE_BACKEND unset defaults to 'memory'
Invalid QUEUE_BACKEND value throws at factory call time with a descriptive error message
tsc --noEmit passes with stub RedisQueue in place

executorDecides: Config object shape and optional params
Stub RedisQueue method bodies
Whether to colocate stub in redis.ts or inline temporarily
escalateToOperator: Runtime backend switching without process restart

phases.title: RedisConnection wrapper + docker-compose fixture
phases.description: Implement RedisConnection in src/queue/connection.ts wrapping ioredis. Add docker-compose.yml with a Redis service for integration tests. Proves ioredis works and test infrastructure is in place before any queue logic is written.
acceptanceCriteria: RedisConnection exported from src/queue/connection.ts with connect(), disconnect(), get client(), and healthCheck() methods
connect() throws if REDIS_URL env var is unset
healthCheck() returns a boolean and never throws
docker-compose.yml starts a Redis instance accessible on a deterministic port
Smoke test confirms RedisConnection connects to the docker-compose Redis and healthCheck() returns true
tsc --noEmit passes

executorDecides: ioredis reconnect strategy and timeout values
Redis image version in docker-compose
Class vs factory function for RedisConnection
Default port number

phases.title: RedisQueue core: enqueue + dequeue (atomic)
phases.description: Replace the stub RedisQueue with a real implementation. Enqueue pushes a serialized job to a Redis list. Dequeue atomically moves a job from the pending list to an inflight sorted set scored by timestamp. Two concurrent dequeue calls must not return the same job.
acceptanceCriteria: enqueue(job) serializes to JSON, pushes to the pending queue key, returns a unique string jobId
dequeue() atomically moves one job from pending list to inflight sorted set; returns deserialized job or null
Two concurrent dequeue() calls on the same queue return distinct jobs (no double-delivery on single dequeue)
size() returns count of pending jobs only (inflight not counted)
Unit tests mock ioredis client and cover enqueue, dequeue, empty-queue null return, and size
tsc --noEmit passes

executorDecides: Redis key naming scheme
Job ID generation strategy (uuid, nanoid, timestamp+random)
Lua script vs LMOVE for atomic dequeue
JSON serialization of job payload
escalateToOperator: Switching to Redis Streams instead of List+SortedSet data model
Storing job metadata in a separate hash

phases.title: At-least-once delivery: ack, nack, visibility timeout
phases.description: Implement ack (removes from inflight), nack (returns to pending), and a visibility timeout daemon that re-queues inflight jobs not acked within the configured window. This is the core at-least-once guarantee.
acceptanceCriteria: ack(jobId) removes the job from the inflight sorted set; no-op if jobId not found
nack(jobId) moves the job from inflight back to the pending list
Visibility timeout daemon re-queues any inflight job whose sorted-set score is older than now - visibilityTimeoutMs
visibilityTimeoutMs is configurable per RedisQueue instance with a documented default
A dequeued, un-acked job is re-deliverable after visibilityTimeoutMs elapses without manual intervention
Unit tests cover ack, nack, and timeout-triggered re-queue using mocked ioredis

executorDecides: Daemon polling interval
setInterval vs recursive setTimeout for daemon lifecycle
Whether nack prepends or appends to pending list
Default visibilityTimeoutMs value
escalateToOperator: Replacing polling with keyspace notifications or Streams consumer groups

phases.title: Retry with exponential backoff + dead-letter queue
phases.description: Extend nack to track per-job retry counts. Jobs below maxRetries are re-queued after an exponential backoff delay. Jobs exceeding maxRetries are promoted to a DLQ key with a 14-day TTL.
acceptanceCriteria: nack() increments a per-job retry counter stored in Redis
If retryCount < maxRetries: job re-enters pending after delay = retryBackoffMs * (multiplier ^ retryCount)
If retryCount >= maxRetries: job is moved to the DLQ key and TTL set to exactly 1209600 seconds (14 days)
maxRetries, retryBackoffMs, and retryBackoffMultiplier are configurable per RedisQueue instance
deadLetterSize() returns the count of entries in the DLQ key
Unit tests cover: first retry (delay applied), final retry (DLQ promotion), DLQ TTL set to 1209600

executorDecides: Retry count storage location (inline in job JSON, separate hash, or sorted set score)
Backoff with or without jitter
DLQ data structure (list vs sorted set)
escalateToOperator: Metrics or alerting on DLQ promotion

phases.title: Integration tests + p99 latency validation
phases.description: End-to-end integration tests against real Redis via docker-compose. Validates the full lifecycle across all phases and measures p99 enqueue latency. This is the acceptance gate before production cutover.
acceptanceCriteria: Integration tests start docker-compose Redis in globalSetup and tear it down in globalTeardown
Full enqueue→dequeue→ack cycle passes against real Redis
nack→retry cycle: job re-enters pending N-1 times, then lands in DLQ on Nth nack
Visibility timeout recovery: dequeued, un-acked job is re-deliverable after timeout without manual intervention
QUEUE_BACKEND=memory and QUEUE_BACKEND=redis both route correctly in the same test run
p99 latency of 1000 sequential enqueue() calls is measured and asserted ≤ 10ms; test fails hard if exceeded

executorDecides: docker-compose start strategy (shell-out vs testcontainers)
Latency measurement implementation (performance.now vs hrtime)
Whether integration tests run in a separate vitest project config
escalateToOperator: CI pipeline changes required to run docker-compose in CI

fullPlan.decompositionRationale: Seven phases map to seven distinct merge-ready work units. p1 locks the API contract first — it has no dependencies and blocks everything else, so ambiguity here must be resolved before parallelism begins. p2 and p3 are the only truly parallel phases: p2 wires the feature flag routing and p3 proves ioredis connectivity plus test infra; both are prerequisites for p4 but neither blocks the other. p4–p6 are sequentially dependent because each layer builds on the previous phase's Redis data model: enqueue/dequeue establishes the pending+inflight structure, ack/nack+visibility timeout adds the delivery guarantee on top of it, and retry/DLQ adds failure-path logic on top of ack/nack. p7 is the integration gate and cannot run until all behavior exists. Collapsing p2+p3 would mix feature flag and infra concerns in one diff and eliminate the only parallel opportunity. Collapsing p4+p5 would exceed six acceptance criteria and span unrelated concerns (atomicity vs. delivery semantics). Splitting further — e.g., separating visibility timeout from ack/nack — would produce phases too small for meaningful review with trivial acceptance criteria.
